# Questions in Livestream 97

Live Stream #97: Certified Genuine by the IAA

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=412" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:52</a> | How are cuneiform names normally rendered into Hebrew, and does a form like Teru to Terah fit the pattern? | He said yes, that kind of shift can fit known transliteration patterns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=640" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:40</a> | Why is Goshen named in Joshua 10 and 11? Is it an error or two places with the same name? | He treated the issue as explainable without forcing a contradiction, likely by geography and naming overlap. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=843" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:03</a> | Was a bed found at Etemenanki with the same dimensions as Og's bed in Deuteronomy 3:11, and what would that mean? | He was not sure about a physical find there, but said comparable ritual bed dimensions are known from cuneiform texts and may support a ritual context for Og's bed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=1122" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:42</a> | Why did Egyptians move away from chariot warfare, and how did that change their military culture? | He said Egypt did not fully abandon chariots until much later; other platforms such as elephants were added, but chariots remained useful for patrol and mobile archery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=1348" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:28</a> | Why did early New Kingdom pharaohs imitate Middle Kingdom art and values? | He compared it to later cultures using older prestige forms: it was a way of rooting authority in a revered past. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=1457" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:17</a> | Why did some ancient writers think Egyptians lived unusually long lives? Did nutrition play a role? | He said adult longevity may have looked high because infant mortality skewed averages, and Egypt's comparatively available medical practice likely mattered more than diet. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=1907" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:47</a> | Did the Late Bronze Age collapse help or hinder the Israelite Exodus, conquest, and settlement? | He said it did not drive the Exodus, but it weakened Egyptian control over Canaan and helped create room for Israelite settlement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=2258" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:38</a> | Do we have evidence for desks in the ancient world, or were people usually writing on their laps and tablets? | He said writing was usually done without desks in the modern sense because scribes needed mobile writing surfaces such as palettes, papyrus, and wax tablets. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=2556" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:36</a> | Is it wrong for Christians to jail someone who has repented, and how should Christians balance justice and love? | He said justice and love are not mutually exclusive; circumstances, public harm, restitution, and genuine repentance all matter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=3079" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:19</a> | What is your opinion of the Dune books? | He said he loves the Dune saga and also likes the Kevin J. Anderson prequel books, though not every Frank Herbert book is equally easy to read. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=3463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:43</a> | Since Sunday school level theology can be bad, how should the Bible be taught in a way that does not go over children's heads? | He said to start with simple but accurate stories, increase detail as children grow, avoid dumbing the Bible down, and treat it as a true story. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=4166" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:26</a> | What evidence shows that Egypt's empire was more of a hegemony? | He pointed especially to the Amarna letters, saying Egypt sought tribute and economic benefit more than direct cultural or administrative control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=4462" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:22</a> | Do Genesis 1:29-30 indicate that all humans and animals were vegetarians before the fall? | He treated vegetarianism as a possible reading or Edenic ideal, but said the text does not give enough information to settle the issue with certainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=4833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:33</a> | What was the average height of an ancient Israelite? | He estimated around five feet two inches, tying short stature mainly to Bronze and Iron Age diet and protein limits. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=4964" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:44</a> | How tall were biblical giants? | He said the answer depends partly on textual tradition and cubit assumptions; Goliath might be read around six or nine feet, with "giant" often meaning unusually tall people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=5330" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:50</a> | If Kaiser argued for a regional flood around the Mediterranean and ancient Near East, how far back would that need to be pushed to be viable? | He said a regional Black Sea or Mediterranean flood would not need to be pushed very far back, roughly around 10,000 years ago, unlike a global-flood model. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=5445" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:45</a> | Are there good, not-too-technical books on reading Isaiah 53 in its ancient Near Eastern context? | He could not name a specific book offhand, but suggested starting with commentaries such as NICOT or Hermeneia. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=5624" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:44</a> | Can someone argue for modern same-sex relationships if Leviticus 18:22 was written in an ancient Near Eastern context for an ancient audience? | He said the argument does not work unless the command is only for that ancient audience, and he pointed to New Testament passages as a major problem for that reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=5872" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:52</a> | What are the meanings of the titles in Isaiah 9:6, such as Everlasting Father, Mighty God, and Prince of Peace? | He said the titles are meaningful in Hebrew, especially "father of eternity," which he distinguished from saying the Son is the Father. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=6407" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:47</a> | What extra-biblical evidence is there for Canaanite evil around the conquest? | He said the evidence is limited, but pointed to Amarna conflict, Egyptian testimony about child sacrifice, Ugaritic parallels, and related regional evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=6595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:55</a> | With early alphabets and similarities between Amorites and Hebrews, could Moses have written the original Torah in an alphabetized early West Semitic dialect? | He said yes, because alphabetic scripts for Semitic languages were already well established before the presumed time of Moses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=6781" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:01</a> | Can you chart out Jonah's journey, including where he was, where Tarshish was, and how the route worked? | He placed Jonah in the northern kingdom, fleeing through Joppa toward Tarshish in Spain, then being thrown overboard early and later going by land toward Nineveh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=7101" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:21</a> | Is Ephraim used as another name for the Messiah, and are there other Messianic names like Immanuel? | He did not know a specific case where Ephraim is synonymous with the Messiah, but said Ephraim can have broader saving or representative associations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=7377" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:57</a> | Can you give a specific example of long ages being used in the Bible to show clan references rather than literal individual lifespans? | He used Exodus 6 and related clan language to argue that the long ages in Moses's genealogy imply abridged clan descent rather than only individual lifespans. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=7974" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:54</a> | What is your take on the view that Christianity is an effeminate religion because of teachings like turning the other cheek and loving enemies? | He rejected the claim, saying Christianity defines strength as restrained, virtuous power rather than weakness or gratuitous violence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=8405" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:05</a> | Why is God called "he" if God is beyond gender? | He said God has no body or gender in that sense, but chose masculine father-language to describe how he relates to humanity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=8671" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:31</a> | How were huge Great Pyramid stones transported over long distances and placed so precisely? | He said they were transported mainly by boat during inundation, then finished and fitted in place rather than fully finished before placement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=9410" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:50</a> | What is your view of hell, such as the Lake of Fire, and is it literal or figurative? | He said he views the Lake of Fire as figurative imagery for real eternal conscious torment caused by God's holiness against sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=9776" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:56</a> | Why are classicists opposed to the idea that Greco-Roman legal traditions had ancient Near Eastern influence? | He attributed much of the resistance to disciplinary bias that treats Greek and Roman civilization as too independent from older Near Eastern sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=9959" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:59</a> | Did Egyptians have sacred animals they would never hunt? | He said no animal was entirely off the hunting list, though some sacred animals may have been hunted and mummified rather than eaten. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=10399" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:19</a> | How did Egyptians deal with dangerous animals like crocodiles and hippos, and were cats more special than dogs? | He said Egyptians hunted dangerous animals extensively; cats and dogs were both important but filled different roles tied to vermin control, hunting, class, and royal display. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=10860" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:00</a> | Did Egyptians think the firmament was solid, or was it more symbolic to them? | He said Egyptian views treated the sky as physically real in important ways, including as the body of Nut and a route for the solar boat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=11167" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:07</a> | Which festival was being observed in John 5: Sukkot, Purim, or something else? | He said Purim was unlikely and, with caveats, leaned toward Hanukkah rather than Sukkot. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=11621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:41</a> | Does Psalm 104:27-30 support reading a carnivore diet into Genesis 1? | He said Psalm 104 is a creation poem and may give some warrant, but its chronology is not precise enough to settle pre-fall diet questions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=12199" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:19</a> | What do you think of reading Isaiah 53:11 as Zerubbabel and Darius throughout the passage? | He was not persuaded, saying the wider language of Isaiah 53 does not fit Zerubbabel well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=12465" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:45</a> | What are your thoughts on qeren in Exodus 34:29, and was Moses's face blistered or was he wearing a horned mask? | He rejected both the blistered-skin and horned-mask readings, preferring that Moses was shining or radiating glory after encountering God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=13237" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:37</a> | Does Genesis 6:3 have a symbolic meaning? | He said it is best read as God giving 120 years before the flood, not as a symbolic meaning or a cap on post-flood human lifespan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=13599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:39</a> | Where did Egyptian hieroglyphs come from: Egypt or somewhere else? | He said Egyptian hieroglyphs came from Egypt, even though Egypt was not the only culture to use hieroglyphic writing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=13867" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:07</a> | What about the newly discovered chamber in the Great Pyramid? | He said the muon or subatomic-particle scanning found a void, and the probed space looks more like a low, unfinished corridor or chamber than a finished room. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/2KzfCJhrZqg?t=14119" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:19</a> | Is there anything cool or any artifacts visible inside the newly discovered pyramid chamber? | He said there were no visible artifacts, but the discovery is still exciting because it shows the Great Pyramid may have more internal features to reveal. |
